codeReview.summary: Feature correctly installs all five required dependencies (three@^0.183.2, @react-three/fiber@^8.18.0, @react-three/drei@^9.122.0, @react-three/postprocessing@^2.19.1, web-audio-beat-detector@^8.2.35) and moves bgvid.mp4 to public/. The worker correctly identified the React 18 vs React 19 peer dependency issue and pinned @react-three/fiber to v8 (the last React 18-compatible major). The commit adds package.json, package-lock.json, and public/bgvid.mp4 — exactly the files needed. Build, typecheck, and tests all pass with no regressions. The bgvid.mp4 move was handled by init.sh which is the correct approach for an asset that was already in the repo root.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered that @react-three/fiber v9+ requires React 19, so React 18-compatible v8 line was used. This version constraint knowledge (React 18 ↔ @react-three/fiber v8, @react-three/drei v9, @react-three/postprocessing v2) is not documented in AGENTS.md or library/ and could be useful for future workers touching Three.js code.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff discoveredIssues[0]: 'Pre-existing: @react-three/fiber v9+ requires React 19. Used v8.18.0 for React 18 compatibility.' Transcript shows worker hit npm peer dep conflict on first install attempt and had to research compatible versions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker noted that the frontend-worker skill says 'When to Return to Orchestrator: Feature requires new npm dependencies not yet installed' — but this feature IS the dependency installation feature. The skill's return-to-orchestrator guidance doesn't account for a feature whose entire purpose is to install dependencies.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The frontend-worker skill says When to Return to Orchestrator: Feature requires new npm dependencies not yet installed — but this feature IS the dependency installation feature, so that guidance doesn't apply here.'

summary: Clean pass. The worker installed all five required npm packages with correct React 18-compatible version pins, moved bgvid.mp4 to public/ via init.sh, and verified build/typecheck/tests all pass unchanged from baseline. No code changes beyond package.json/package-lock.json and the asset file. No issues found.
